programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors next hears from the director of the day care where Bill and Melanie McGuire's sons attended.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.desc: An analyst examined eight computers and phones during Bill McGuire's murder investigation.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.desc: The jury learns of more Internet searches done on the family computer before Bill McGuire died.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors heard more testimony from a criminalist who worked this case. Investigators say they found overwhelming DNA evidence that linked the defendant to the death of her stepdad, Tom Merriman. Next, another criminalist who examined evidence linked to Merriman's death explained to the jury how much of the defendant's DNA profile they uncovered. More likely than not was how DNA test results came back, claiming to show Jade Janks' involvement in her stepdad's death.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors listened to the expert testimony of the deputy medical examiner. Dr. Greg Pizarro did the autopsy on Tom Merriman and made his conclusion about the cause and manner of his death. The cause of Merriman's death was an overdose from a drug typically used for the short-term treatment of insomnia. Jurors would learn later what medications Merriman was on and what he was allegedly abusing.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: The how and why Tom Merriman died was brought up for jurors. On the stand was Dr. Greg Pizarro who performed his autopsy to find the cause and manner of his death. Autopsy photos that were too graphic for television cameras were presented to jurors. They listened to Dr. Pizarro describing in detail the injuries Merriman sustained at the time of his death. Prosecutors argued that Merriman's overdose was not his own doing, claiming instead that he was drugged.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: The cause of death in this case was a drug overdose, but the manner of death was homicide.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: Ezra McCandless frantically banged on the front door of a remote farmhouse, barefoot, and covered in Alex Woodworth's blood. That was after she fatally stabbed him and left him in her car.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: Police had a lot to process at the crime scene where Alex Woodworth's body had been found. You had foot and tire impressions, Alex's smashed phone, blood, the murder weapon found along the road and Ezra McCandless's car stuck in the mud.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Body Hanging Out The Backseat Of A Car
programme.desc: Crime scene investigators began processing the area where the body of Alex Woodworth was found. He had 16 stab wounds and half of his body was hanging out of the back seat of Ezra McCandless's car.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: The Car And The Crime Scene
programme.desc: Photos of Ezra McCandless were presented to jurors. At the time Alex Woodworth was killed, Ezra was an aspiring artist. In fact, she would paint the body and trunk of her car with her art.

programme.desc: A young woman went missing from her home on a bright spring morning. After several days of searching, agents suspected a paroled criminal who frequented her health club may have abducted her.

programme.desc: Police led an intense search and relied on forensics when the mother of two young children went missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Security cameras in a casino tracked a young woman's movements until shortly before she disappeared. She was never seen again.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Nancy Newman and her two daughters are found brutally murdered. The crime scene has no shortage of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A driver said he couldn't have hit and killed a pedestrian because his Jeep had been sold months ago.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The prime suspect had a criminal record and his driver's license was found at the scene of a brutal double homicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police recovered the car of a missing man, they expected to find his body - but it wasn't there.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In this international case of extortion, murder and stolen identities, scam-artist Albert J. Walker assumes the identity of co-worker Ronald Platt.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Native American woman was brutally killed in the desert of New Mexico, and the crime scene was rich in forensic evidence: tire tracks, shoe impressions and even the murder weapons.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter is the victim of a well-planned crime. Investigators suspect two weapons and two killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a trio of unsolved murders occurred in Wichita Falls, Texas. Later, a fourth murder from the same time period provided the police with far more than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was shot to death in her Connecticut driveway. Now police must determine if love had turned deadly.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a history of heart disease. Rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teenager went missing after an evening of horseback riding. Her body was found a month later, three miles from her home. The killer unknowingly left trace evidence behind - tiny but unmistakable clues which pointed to him and him alone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1969, phone operator Diane Maxwell is murdered. Her brother promised he'd find out who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott, police use a garbage bag to connect a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-year-old boy was rushed to the hospital suffering from brain seizures and breathing difficulties.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One warm summer afternoon, the town of Verona, Wisconsin faced its first triple homicide. To solve the case, investigators had to delve into the world of high rollers and offshore betting.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Christina Sanoubane was murdered in her Cedar Rapids apartment after moving away from her abusive boyfriend.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In rural Canada, Dr. John Schneeberger is accused of sedating and sexually assaulting one of his female patients and his stepdaughter. DNA tests demonstrate that the doctor is innocent, but the patient insists that he sedated and raped her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on a golf course. The grass on the course was so distinctive, it had evidentiary value.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial rapist is on the loose in a Texas town. No one knew where he'd strike next, but the audacity of his attacks and the escalation of violence against his victims made finding him imperative.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a tragic twist of fate, just days after the woman sold her home and moved to a modest trailer, a fire took both the trailer and her life. But the autopsy proved this was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on the bedroom floor of her apartment. The crime scene yielded little of value and investigators wondered if they would find enough evidence to make a case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the head chef of a historic Philadelphia restaurant was found dead, investigators interviewed the usual suspects: family, friends and coworkers. As they sifted through the evidence, police uncovered a chilling tale of debt and deceit.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, Shannon Melendi disappeared while at Emory University. Her disappearance remained a mystery for ten years, until new scientific testing cast a different light on Colvin "Butch" Hinton.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared and her husband became the prime suspect especially after police found a huge bloodstain on the carpet of their bedroom. Complex DNA testing proved it was the wife's blood. Now all they had to do was find her body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, Maine resident Pearl Smith is missing after an argument with her husband Bill Bruns. But when an investigation turns up a blood trail that leads to the couple's basement, police are sure they'll find the victim's body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is shot to death in her store just one day before she is to testify against the man accused of robbing her. The robber becomes the prime suspect, but he has a solid alibi.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Katie Poirier is stymied by the lack of a body. Finally, investigators use a single tooth found among some charred remains to identify her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2004, nursing student Tamika Huston went missing from her Spartanburg, South Carolina home. A tip led detectives to her car, where they found an unknown house key that could help solve the case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On a cold December night in 1993, Rose Larner stopped in a convenience store on her way to her boyfriend's house. She was never seen or heard from again. A tiny clue found years later revealed a tragic tale of drugs, romance and revenge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young couple decided to celebrate their first wedding anniversary with a camping trip to Mount Hood. During the trip, the husband was shot and killed. Later, his wife told two different versions of the events which led to his death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teenager went missing after an evening of horseback riding. Her body was found a month later, three miles from her home. The killer unknowingly left trace evidence behind - tiny but unmistakable clues which pointed to him and him alone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When more than 700 Oregonians contracted food poisoning from a unique strain of salmonella in 1984, investigators uncovered a secret plot to influence local elections.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a hit-and-run accident claimed the life of a high school athlete, everyone in town mourned his passing. Finding the killer was a long shot at best.

programme.title: Forensic Files
